devanagari: महारासवर्णनं परीक्षित्कृत शंकायाः शुकद्वारा समाधानं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्थं भगवतो गोप्यः श्रुत्वा वाचः सुपेशलाः । जहुर्विरहजं तापं तदङ...
english_devnagari: mahārāsavarṇanaṃ parīkṣitkṛta śaṃkāyāḥ śukadvārā samādhānaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca itthaṃ bhagavato gopyaḥ śrutvā vācaḥ supeśalāḥ jahurvirahajaṃ tāpaṃ tadaṅgopacitāśiṣaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Listening to these soft fascinating words of the Lord, the Gopīs forgot the agony caused by separation from him and their bliss was enhanced by persona] contact with him, Oh dear king.

devanagari: तत्रारभत गोविन्दो रासक्रीडामनुव्रतैः । स्त्रीरत्‍नैरन्वितः प्रीतैः अन्योन्याबद्धबाहुभिः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrārabhata govindo rāsakrīḍāmanuvrataiḥ strīrat‍nairanvitaḥ prītaiḥ anyonyābaddhabāhubhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ prī class 9 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: There (on the sandy bank of the Yamunā) Lord Govinda inaugurated his Rāsa Krīḍā[1] (the play called Rāsa) with those jewel-like damsels (of superb beauty) who were so devoted and affectionate to him and who formed a circle with their arms interlocked (with those of Kṛṣṇa).[2]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
SG. quotes from Bharata’s Nāṭya-śāstra: The dance in which a number of female dancers dance with their hands interlocked in the company of men who place their arms round the neck of the women-partners:
naṭair gṛhīta-kaṇṭhīnām anyānyāttakara-śriyāṃ /
nartakīnām bhaved rāso maṇḍalībhūya nartatām //

Footnote [2]:
SG. quotes Bilva-maṅgala which describes how Kṛṣṇa accompanied each Gopi as her dance-mate: “A circle of dancers was formed with Kṛṣṇa between two dancing ladies and a lady between two Kṛṣṇas. In the middle of such a circle of dancers Kṛṣṇa stood and played upon His flute.
aṅganām aṅganām antarā mādhavo /
mādhavaṃ mādhavaṃ cā'ntareṇāṅganā //
ittham ākalpite maṇḍale madhyagaḥ /
saṃjagau veṇunā devakī-nandanaḥ //
This is possible only to Bhagavān Kṛṣṇa with His yogamāyā. Hence the significance of the words bhagavān and yogamāyām upāśritaḥ in Supra 10.29.1.
Vide infra V. 20 which states that Kṛṣṇa assumed as many forms as there were the cowherd-women.
kṛtvā tāvantam ātmānaṃ yāvatīr gopa-yoṣitaḥ /

devanagari: रासोत्सवः सम्प्रवृत्तो गोपीमण्डलमण्डितः । योगेश्वरेण कृष्णेन तासां मध्ये द्वयोर्द्वयोः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: rāsotsavaḥ sampravṛtto gopīmaṇḍalamaṇḍitaḥ yogeśvareṇa kṛṣṇena tāsāṃ madhye dvayordvayoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ maṇḍ ]

translation: Then commenced formally the festive dance called rāsa, beautified splendidly by the (moving) circle of Gopīs with Kṛṣṇa, the great Master of Yoga of inconceivable mystic powers (assumed as many forms as there were Gopīs and) entered in between every two Gopīs and with his arm placed around the neck of the adjacent Gopī, made each of the damsels consider to be nearest to him (and being embraced by him).

devanagari: प्रविष्टेन गृहीतानां कण्ठे स्वनिकटं स्त्रियः । यं मन्येरन् नभस्तावद् विमानशतसङ्कुलम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: praviṣṭena gṛhītānāṃ kaṇṭhe svanikaṭaṃ striyaḥ yaṃ manyeran nabhastāvad vimānaśatasaṅkulam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative middle third plural], [optative passive third plural]

translation: (As soon as Lord Kṛṣṇa thought of beginning the rāsakrīḍā) there thronged in the sky hundreds of heavenly cars of celestials along with their wives whose minds were carried away with curiosity (of seeing the rasa dance).

devanagari: दिवौकसां सदाराणां औत्सुक्यापहृतात्मनाम् । ततो दुन्दुभयो नेदुः निपेतुः पुष्पवृष्टयः । जगुर्गन्धर्वपतयः सस्त्रीकास्तद् यशोऽमलम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: divaukasāṃ sadārāṇāṃ autsukyāpahṛtātmanām tato dundubhayo neduḥ nipetuḥ puṣpavṛṣṭayaḥ jagurgandharvapatayaḥ sastrīkāstad yaśo
translation: At that time, kettle-drums were sounded and showers of flowers fell. The heads of Gandharvas accompanied by their consorts sang the noble and hallowing glory of the Lord.

devanagari: वलयानां नूपुराणां किङ्किणीनां च योषिताम् । सप्रियाणामभूत् शब्दः तुमुलो रासमण्डले ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: valayānāṃ nūpurāṇāṃ kiṅkiṇīnāṃ ca yoṣitām sapriyāṇāmabhūt śabdaḥ tumulo rāsamaṇḍale
translation: There arose in the ring of rāsa (dancers), the (orchestral) din of the jingling bangles and anklets and the tinkling of the small bells of girdles of the ladies accompanied by their darling Lord.

devanagari: तत्रातिशुशुभे ताभिः भगवान् देवकीसुतः । मध्ये मणीनां हैमानां महामरकतो यथा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrātiśuśubhe tābhiḥ bhagavān devakīsutaḥ madhye maṇīnāṃ haimānāṃ mahāmarakato yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Exceedingly beautiful shone the glorious Lord, the son of Devakī, in the midst of those pairs of damsels like unto a highly precious emerald[3] embedded in between a pair of gold beads.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
SG. explains that although Kṛṣṇa was of sapphire-blue complexion, the brilliant-golden complexion of the Gopīs, He embraced, made Him appear like emerald.

devanagari: पादन्यासैर्भुजविधुतिभिः । सस्मितैर्भ्रूविलासैः । भज्यन्मध्यैश्चलकुचपटैः । कुण्डलैर्गण्डलोलैः । स्विद्यन्मुख्यः कवररसना । ग्रन्थयः कृष्ण...
english_devnagari: pādanyāsairbhujavidhutibhiḥ sasmitairbhrūvilāsaiḥ bhajyanmadhyaiścalakucapaṭaiḥ kuṇḍalairgaṇḍalolaiḥ svidyanmukhyaḥ kavararasanā granthayaḥ kṛṣṇavadhvo gāyantyastaṃ taḍita iva tā meghacakre virejuḥ

translation: With their measured treads of feet and (artistic) movements of their hands (in dancing), with their smiles and (gracefully) amorous dance of eyebrows, with bending waists and shaking breasts, with the fluttering of their garments, with ear-rings rocking on their cheeks, with drops of perspiration trikling down their face and with their braids of hair and girdles getting loosened (in the course of dancing), the dancing-mates of Kṛṣṇa glorified the Lord in songs and appeared beautiful like flashes of lightening illuminating a circle of clouds (as Kṛṣṇa assumed as many forms as the Gopīs in that circular movement of rāsa).

devanagari: उच्चैर्जगुर्नृत्यमाना रक्तकण्ठ्यो रतिप्रियाः । कृष्णाभिमर्शमुदिता यद्‍गीतेनेदमावृतम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: uccairjagurnṛtyamānā raktakaṇṭhyo ratipriyāḥ kṛṣṇābhimarśamuditā yad‍gītenedamāvṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb]

translation: [4] In the course of dancing, the Gopīs, enamoured of loving devotion to Kṛṣṇa and thrilled with delight at the touch of Kṛṣṇa, sang at the pitch of their voices sweetened with different musical inodes (rāgas) filling thus the whole universe with the music (of their song).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Commentators quote names of Gopīs. These names of the Gopīs involved in the dance are imaginary-unsupported by the text of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa

devanagari: काचित् समं मुकुन्देन स्वरजातीरमिश्रिताः । उन्निन्ये पूजिता तेन प्रीयता साधु साध्विति । तदेव ध्रुवमुन्निन्ये तस्यै मानं च बह्वदात् ॥ १०...
english_devnagari: kācit samaṃ mukundena svarajātīramiśritāḥ unninye pūjitā tena prīyatā sādhu sādhviti tadeva dhruvamunninye tasyai mānaṃ ca bahvadāt

translation: A certain Gopī sang in chorus with Kṛṣṇa in a higher pitch. Even though her notes of the gamut (svara-jāti) did not harmonize with that of Kṛṣṇa, she was applauded by him with the words “Well-done”, as he was pleased with her performance. When she proceeded to reach her highest pitch to the measured beat (tāla) well-known as Dhruva (pada), Kṛṣṇa showed great regard for her.

devanagari: काचिद् रासपरिश्रान्ता पार्श्वस्थस्य गदाभृतः । जग्राह बाहुना स्कन्धं श्लथद्वलयमल्लिका ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kācid rāsapariśrāntā pārśvasthasya gadābhṛtaḥ jagrāha bāhunā skandhaṃ ślathadvalayamallikā

translation: Thoroughly exhausted by (participation in) the rāsa dance and with herbangles slipping (from her wrists) and jasmine flowers dropping (from her braid), another Gopī caught hold by her arm, the shoulder of Kṛṣṇa who was standing by (i.e. flung her arms round him).

devanagari: तत्रैकांसगतं बाहुं कृष्णस्योत्पलसौरभम् । चन्दनालिप्तमाघ्राय हृष्टरोमा चुचुम्ब ह ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tatraikāṃsagataṃ bāhuṃ kṛṣṇasyotpalasaurabham candanāliptamāghrāya hṛṣṭaromā cucumba ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: (It is reported that) another Gopī smelt how Kṛṣṇa’s arm placed on her shoulder, was fragrant like a lily and was anointed with sandal paste, (and losing herself) she (actually) kissed it and was thrilled with it (with her hair standing on their ends).

devanagari: कस्याश्चित् नाट्यविक्षिप्त कुण्डलत्विषमण्डितम् । गण्डं गण्डे सन्दधत्याः अदात्ताम्बूलचर्वितम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kasyāścit nāṭyavikṣipta kuṇḍalatviṣamaṇḍitam gaṇḍaṃ gaṇḍe sandadhatyāḥ adāttāmbūlacarvitam

translation: To another Gopī who rested on Kṛṣṇa’s cheek her own which was glowing with the brilliance of her own earrings rocking in the course of dancing Kṛṣṇa gave his halfchewed betel.

devanagari: नृत्यती गायती काचित् कूजन् नूपुरमेखला । पार्श्वस्थाच्युतहस्ताब्जं श्रान्ताधात् स्तनयोः शिवम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: nṛtyatī gāyatī kācit kūjan nūpuramekhalā pārśvasthācyutahastābjaṃ śrāntādhāt stanayoḥ śivam

translation: Another Gopi, standing by his side was fatigued with singing and dancing, making all the while a jingling sound of her anklets and girdle (while dancing) pressed to her bosom his blissful lotus-hand.

devanagari: गोप्यो लब्ध्वाच्युतं कान्तं श्रिय एकान्तवल्लभम् । गृहीतकण्ठ्यस्तद्दोर्भ्यां गायन्त्यस्तं विजह्रिरे ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gopyo labdhvācyutaṃ kāntaṃ śriya ekāntavallabham gṛhītakaṇṭhyastaddorbhyāṃ gāyantyastaṃ vijahrire

[nominative single from √ kaṇṭh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kaṇṭh class 10 verb]

translation: Obtaining Acyuta (the immortal God—Kṛṣṇa), the sole Darling of the goddess Śrī (Lakṣmī) as their beloved, the cowherd women with his arms round their neck extolled him in song and played (danced) with him.

devanagari: कर्णोत्पलालकविटङ्ककपोलघर्म । वक्त्रश्रियो वलयनूपुरघोषवाद्यैः । गोप्यः समं भगवता ननृतुः स्वकेश । स्रस्तस्रजो भ्रमरगायकरासगोष्ठ्याम् ॥ १...
english_devnagari: karṇotpalālakaviṭaṅkakapolagharma vaktraśriyo valayanūpuraghoṣavādyaiḥ gopyaḥ samaṃ bhagavatā nanṛtuḥ svakeśa srastasrajo bhramaragāyakarāsagoṣṭhyām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ val ], [vocative single from √ val ]
[instrumental plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ vad ]
[instrumental plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ vad ]

translation: The Gopa women with the beauty of their face enhanced by lotuses adorning their ears, cheeks beautified with their curly locks of hair, and drops of perspiration, with wreaths of flowers dropping from their (braids of) hair, danced with the glorious Lord accompanied with the musical instruments in the form of jingling bangles and anklets, at the ring of the rāsa dance where humming blackbees acted as songsters.

devanagari: एवं परिष्वङ्गकराभिमर्श । स्निग्धेक्षणोद्दामविलासहासैः । रेमे रमेशो व्रजसुन्दरीभिः । यथार्भकः स्वप्रतिबिम्बविभ्रमः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ pariṣvaṅgakarābhimarśa snigdhekṣaṇoddāmavilāsahāsaiḥ reme rameśo vrajasundarībhiḥ yathārbhakaḥ svapratibimbavibhramaḥ

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: [5] Kṛṣṇa; the Lord of goddess Lakṣmī, sported with the beauties of Vraja by embracing them, pressing their palms, winsome oglings with free amorous gestures and hearty (loud) laughter, even as a child would play with its own reflection (in the mirror).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Padaratnāvalī God is absolutely independent and above all prescriptions and prohibitions and can violate all canons of conduct!

devanagari: तदङ्गसङ्गप्रमुदाकुलेन्द्रियाः । केशान् दूकूलं कुचपट्टिकां वा । नाञ्जः प्रतिव्योढुमलं व्रजस्त्रियो । विस्रस्तमालाभरणाः कुरूद्वह ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tadaṅgasaṅgapramudākulendriyāḥ keśān dūkūlaṃ kucapaṭṭikāṃ vā nāñjaḥ prativyoḍhumalaṃ vrajastriyo visrastamālābharaṇāḥ kurūdvaha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Their senses were so overwhelmed with ecstatic joy. at the contact with that Divine Personality that the women of Vraja whose wreaths and ornaments were loosened and falling, were too much beside themselves (with joy) to re-adjust their dishevelled locks of hair, their garments and brassiers, Oh Prominent Kuru!

devanagari: कृष्णविक्रीडितं वीक्ष्य मुमुहुः खेचरस्त्रियः । कामार्दिताः शशाङ्कश्च सगणो विस्मितोऽभवत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇavikrīḍitaṃ vīkṣya mumuhuḥ khecarastriyaḥ kāmārditāḥ śaśāṅkaśca sagaṇo vismito
translation: While witnessing Kṛṣṇa’s love sports, the celestial, damsels were smitten with love and fainted. And even the Moon-god along with his retinue (of stars or nakṣatras) was too much amazed (to proceed with his movements and halted).

devanagari: कृत्वा तावन्तमात्मानं यावतीर्गोपयोषितः । रेमे स भगवान् ताभिः आत्मारामोऽपि लीलया ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtvā tāvantamātmānaṃ yāvatīrgopayoṣitaḥ reme sa bhagavān tābhiḥ ātmārāmo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Though a reveller in his own Supreme bliss, the Lord assumed as many forms as there were Gopīs and delightfully disported[6] with each of them.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Padaratnāvalī reads ‘rarāma’. The Parasmaipada form indicates the aloofness of the Lord in that rāsa:
rarāmeti vyatyayeṇa para-dārābhimarśaṇena doṣo nāsti iti vakṣyamāṇam artham ihaiva sūcayati

devanagari: तासां अतिविहारेण श्रान्तानां वदनानि सः । प्रामृजत् करुणः प्रेम्णा शन्तमेनाङ्ग पाणिना ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tāsāṃ ativihāreṇa śrāntānāṃ vadanāni saḥ prāmṛjat karuṇaḥ premṇā śantamenāṅga pāṇinā
translation: The merciful Lord wiped out affectionately, the sweat off their faces with his most blissful comforting hand as they were fatigued with overdancing with him, Oh ḍear king.

devanagari: गोप्यः स्फुरत्पुरटकुण्डलकुन्तलत्विड् । गण्डश्रिया सुधितहासनिरीक्षणेन । मानं दधत्य ऋषभस्य जगुः कृतानि । पुण्यानि तत्कररुहस्पर्शप्रमोदाः...
english_devnagari: gopyaḥ sphuratpuraṭakuṇḍalakuntalatviḍ gaṇḍaśriyā sudhitahāsanirīkṣaṇena mānaṃ dadhatya ṛṣabhasya jaguḥ kṛtāni puṇyāni tatkararuhasparśapramodāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb]

translation: Adoring the Supreme Lord with the splendour of their cheeks enhanced by the brilliant gold ear-rings and curly locks of hair and with glances accompanied with nectar-like smiles, the Gopīs who (despite their fatigue in the rāsa dance) became enraptured by the touch of his finger-nails, burst out in singing his hallowing deeds.[7]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
These songs suggested the conclusion of the rāsa (Ve.)

devanagari: ताभिर्युतः श्रममपोहितुमङ्गसङ्ग । घृष्टस्रजः स कुचकुङ्कुमरञ्जितायाः । गन्धर्वपालिभिरनुद्रुत आविशद् वा । श्रान्तो गजीभिरिभराडिव भिन्नसेत...
english_devnagari: tābhiryutaḥ śramamapohitumaṅgasaṅga ghṛṣṭasrajaḥ sa kucakuṅkumarañjitāyāḥ gandharvapālibhiranudruta āviśad vā śrānto gajībhiribharāḍiva bhinnasetuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb]
[ablative single from √ rañj ], [genitive single from √ rañj ]
[nominative plural from √ śrā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śrā class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ śrā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śrā class 2 verb]

translation: Like unto a king-elephant breaking down the dams and entering water in the company of she-elephants, the exhausted[8] Lord Kṛṣṇa who had transgressed the restraints (laid down by the Vedas and public decorum) became desirous of getting over the fatigue, and in the company of the Gopīs, entered the waters of the Yamunā (where he was) closely followed by black-bees (humming melodiously) like the chiefs of celestial songsters, the Gandharvas, (as they were) allured by his garland (of Kunda flowers) squeezed by contact with the persons of the Gopīs (while embracing etc.) and was coloured with saffron applied by them to their breasts.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
SG. imagines that this adj. is used to indicate that the beauty of Kṛṣṇa’s countenance was enhanced by droplets of swea [sweat?] appearing on it through fatigue.

devanagari: सोऽम्भस्यलं युवतिभिः परिषिच्यमानः । प्रेम्णेक्षितः प्रहसतीभिरितस्ततोऽङ्ग । वैमानिकैः कुसुमवर्षिभिरीड्यमानो । रेमे स्वयं स्वरतिरत्र गजे...
english_devnagari: so premṇekṣitaḥ prahasatībhiritastato vaimānikaiḥ kusumavarṣibhirīḍyamāno reme svayaṃ svaratiratra gajendralīlaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ īḍ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ īḍ class 2 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Oh dear Parīkṣit! The Lord, who ever revels in his own blissful state, sported here like a king-elephant as he was profusely sprinkled over with water on all sides by the youthful damsels who were casting amorous glances at him, and were heartily laughing, while he was being praised by gods who were showering on him flowers from their aerial cars.

devanagari: ततश्च कृष्णोपवने जलस्थल । प्रसूनगन्धानिलजुष्टदिक्तटे । चचार भृङ्गप्रमदागणावृतो । यथा मदच्युद् द्विरदः करेणुभिः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca kṛṣṇopavane jalasthala prasūnagandhānilajuṣṭadiktaṭe cacāra bhṛṅgapramadāgaṇāvṛto yathā madacyud dviradaḥ kareṇubhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Like unto a young elephant exuding temporal fluid sauntering in the company of female elephants, the Lord, surrounded by a swarm of black-bees and a bevy of young ladies, roved in a grove on the bank of the Yamunā, where blew on all sides a gentle breeze bearing the fragrance of acquatic flowers as well as of the flowers on lands.

devanagari: एवं शशाङ्कांशुविराजिता निशाः । स सत्यकामोऽनुरताबलागणः । सिषेव आत्मन्यवरुद्धसौरतः । सर्वाः शरत्काव्यकथारसाश्रयाः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ śaśāṅkāṃśuvirājitā niśāḥ sa satyakāmo siṣeva ātmanyavaruddhasaurataḥ sarvāḥ śaratkāvyakathārasāśrayāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: In this way, the Lord of accomplished desires to whom the bevy of young damsels were deeply attached and devoted, enjoyed all those nights (without losing a single minute without enjoyment—the night[9] illuminated by the rays of the moon and which formed the basis for all charming poetical works specially appropriate for autumnal season—but he controlled all his energy within himself (such was his control over Eros.)
purport:
Footnote [9]:
The plural number indicates that throughout His Kiśora period (from 11 to 15 years of age), Kṛṣṇa enjoyed the rāsa-dance every night—SG. This is a better interpretation than imagining that Kṛṣṇa extended that particular night for many days while Gopīs felt it to be a single night.

devanagari: श्रीपरीक्षिदुवाच । संस्थापनाय धर्मस्य प्रशमायेतरस्य च । अवतीर्णो हि भगवानंन् अंशेन जगदीश्वरः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīparīkṣiduvāca saṃsthāpanāya dharmasya praśamāyetarasya ca avatīrṇo hi bhagavānaṃn aṃśena jagadīśvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
King Parīkṣit said:
The Almighty Lord of the Universe along with his aṃśa-part (Balarāma) incarnated here to establish Dharma (righteous way of life) and the suppression of the vile way of life.

devanagari: स कथं धर्मसेतूनां वक्ता कर्ताभिरक्षिता । प्रतीपमाचरद् ब्रह्मन् परदाराभिमर्शनम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa kathaṃ dharmasetūnāṃ vaktā kartābhirakṣitā pratīpamācarad brahman paradārābhimarśanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: He is the Promulgator of the moral order. He is the ideal model of ethical standard and the Protector thereof. How did he act in direct contravention of it, in seducing sensually the wives of others?

devanagari: आप्तकामो यदुपतिः कृतवान् वै जुगुप्सितम् । किमभिप्राय एतन्नः संशयं छिन्धि सुव्रत ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: āptakāmo yadupatiḥ kṛtavān vai jugupsitam kimabhiprāya etannaḥ saṃśayaṃ chindhi suvrata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ gup ], [accusative single from √ gup ]

translation: Oh holy sage of sacred vows! Be pleased to resolve our doubt as to with what intention the Lord of Yadus who is said to have accomplished all his desires, perpetrated such censurable deed.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । धर्मव्यतिक्रमो दृष्ट ईश्वराणां च साहसम् । तेजीयसां न दोषाय वह्नेः सर्वभुजो यथा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca dharmavyatikramo dṛṣṭa īśvarāṇāṃ ca sāhasam tejīyasāṃ na doṣāya vahneḥ sarvabhujo yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka explained:
Transgression of the canons of righteousness and over-adventurism are found to have been committed by mighty rulers. But that does not amount to a blemish for a noble heroic soul, as in the case of omnivorous fire (which does not get polluted by consuming impure things).

devanagari: नैतत्समाचरेज्जातु मनसापि ह्यनीश्वरः । विनश्यत्याचरन्मौढ्याद् यथारुद्रोऽब्धिजं विषम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: naitatsamācarejjātu manasāpi hyanīśvaraḥ vinaśyatyācaranmauḍhyād yathārudro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [locative single from √ śā class 4 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: An incapable weakling (dependent on his body) should never commit this (transgression) even mentally. A person imitating him foolishly meets destruction like a person other than Rudra (but imitating Rudra) does by swallowing the (Halāhala) poison churned out of the sea.

devanagari: ईश्वराणां वचः सत्यं तथैवाचरितं क्वचित् । तेषां यत् स्ववचोयुक्तं बुद्धिमांस्तत् समाचरेत् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: īśvarāṇāṃ vacaḥ satyaṃ tathaivācaritaṃ kvacit teṣāṃ yat svavacoyuktaṃ buddhimāṃstat samācaret

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: The words of the possessors of divine wisdom and might are always correct and authoritative, but not necessarily, so their acts (which may be emulated on certain occasions only). Hence an intelligent person should practice such course (of conduct) which is consistent with their own precepts.[10]
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: Therefore, an intelligent person should follow those acts of God which are consistent with His precepts. OR A prudent man should not emulate the acts of God which are against His precepts.
Padaratnāvalī: An intelligent person should not practice what is said or regarded as contrary to Vedic injuctions.

devanagari: कुशलाचरितेनैषां इह स्वार्थो न विद्यते । विपर्ययेण वानर्थो निरहङ्कारिणां प्रभो ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kuśalācaritenaiṣāṃ iha svārtho na vidyate viparyayeṇa vānartho nirahaṅkāriṇāṃ prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 5 verb]
[vocative single from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ u class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ u class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ u class 5 verb]

translation: Such authoritative persons who are devoid of ego ṭahaṃkāra) have no selfish ends to serve by virtuous behaviour or have no stakes to lose by acting to the contrary.

devanagari: किमुताखिलसत्त्वानां तिर्यङ् मर्त्यदिवौकसाम् । ईशितुश्चेशितव्यानां कुशलाकुशलान्वयः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kimutākhilasattvānāṃ tiryaṅ martyadivaukasām īśituśceśitavyānāṃ kuśalākuśalānvayaḥ
translation: Need it be told that the Lord, the controller of all beings like the sub-human creation (birds, beasts etc.), human beings and celestials who, being fit to be, governed by him (and are subject to his control), is not concerned with piety or impiety of the acts (the moral code of conduct of ordinary beings is not applicable to the Almighty).

devanagari: यत्पादपङ्कजपरागनिषेवतृप्ता । योगप्रभावविधुताखिलकर्मबन्धाः । स्वैरं चरन्ति मुनयोऽपि न नह्यमानाः । तस्येच्छयाऽत्तवपुषः कुत एव बन्धः ॥ ३५...
english_devnagari: yatpādapaṅkajaparāganiṣevatṛptā yogaprabhāvavidhutākhilakarmabandhāḥ svairaṃ caranti munayo tasyecchayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ tṛp class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ tṛp class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ tṛp class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nah class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ nah class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nah class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ nah class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ nah class 4 verb]

translation: The sages who are completely satisfied (and have become desireless) by serving the pollen-like dust of his lotus-feet and who have absolutely freed themselves by shaking off all the bondage of karmas by virtue of their Yogic Powers, behave according to their free will and are not bound (by their acts). How could there be any bondage to the glorious Lord who has assumed a personality at his own free will?

devanagari: गोपीनां तत्पतीनां च सर्वेषामेव देहिनाम् । योऽन्तश्चरति सोऽध्यक्षः क्रीडनेनेह देहभाक् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: gopīnāṃ tatpatīnāṃ ca sarveṣāmeva dehinām yo
translation: He who dwells not only in these Gopīs but also in their husbands—nay in all embodied beings (as their Inner Controller), and who presides over all these, has as a sport, assumed the form of Kṛṣṇa in this world.

devanagari: अनुग्रहाय भूतानां मानुषं देहमास्थितः । भजते तादृशीः क्रीड याः श्रुत्वा तत्परो भवेत् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: anugrahāya bhūtānāṃ mānuṣaṃ dehamāsthitaḥ bhajate tādṛśīḥ krīḍa yāḥ śrutvā tatparo bhavet
translation: It is for showering Grace on all creatures that the Lord has put on a human form and participated in those sportful activities (like the rāsa krīḍā). By listening to these, a person may become attached to him exclusively (as a devotee).

devanagari: नासूयन् खलु कृष्णाय मोहितास्तस्य मायया । मन्यमानाः स्वपार्श्वस्थान् स्वान् स्वान् दारान् व्रजौकसः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: nāsūyan khalu kṛṣṇāya mohitāstasya māyayā manyamānāḥ svapārśvasthān svān svān dārān vrajaukasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ūy class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ūy class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ muh ], [vocative plural from √ muh ]
[nominative plural from √ muh ], [vocative plural from √ muh ], [accusative plural from √ muh ]
[nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: The male inhabitants of Vraja were so much spellbound by the deluding potency of the Lord that they always considered their wives ever present by their sides and they never felt any jealousy against Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: ब्रह्मरात्र उपावृत्ते वासुदेवानुमोदिताः । अनिच्छन्त्यो ययुर्गोप्यः स्वगृहान् भगवत्प्रियाः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmarātra upāvṛtte vāsudevānumoditāḥ anicchantyo yayurgopyaḥ svagṛhān bhagavatpriyāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: At the approach of the Brāhma muhūrta (i. e. dawn) the Gopīs, the beloveds of the glorious Lord, returned reluctantly to their homes with the permission[11] of the Lord.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
SG.—anu-moditāḥ—who were all the while—(every minute of that night)—filled with joy by Kṛṣṇa (hence their reluctance to return).

devanagari: विक्रीडितं व्रजवधूभिरिदं च विष्णोः । श्रद्धान्वितोऽनुश्रृणुयादथ वर्णयेद्यः । भक्तिं परां भगवति प्रतिलभ्य कामं । हृद्रोगमाश्वपहिनोत्यचि...
english_devnagari: vikrīḍitaṃ vrajavadhūbhiridaṃ ca viṣṇoḥ śraddhānvito bhaktiṃ parāṃ bhagavati pratilabhya kāmaṃ hṛdrogamāśvapahinotyacireṇa dhīraḥ
translation:
He who reverentially listens to and rehearses the account of the amorous sport of Viṣṇu (i. e. his incarnation Kṛṣṇa) with the damsels of Vraja, conceives intense devotion to the Almightly Lord, attains spiritual wisdom and selfcontrol, and soon overcomes the maladies of the heart (like lust and other passions).
